tickets.title: Build the OpenCode integration for the ADLC toolkit
tickets.body:
Build a first-party OpenCode integration for the @adlc/* toolkit, replacing the planned-only stub at docs/integrations/opencode.md. Mirror the closest precedent, adlc-pi (plugins/adlc-pi/index.ts) — an in-process terminal coding-agent plugin — and match the rail-enforcement contract of BOTH sibling hooks exactly (plugins/adlc-pi/index.ts and plugins/adlc-codex/hooks/adlc-rails-guard.mjs). OpenCode is the sst/opencode terminal AI coding agent. Do NOT reimplement any @adlc/* gate; wire OpenCode's native plugin hook to invoke / mirror the existing gate logic.

=== RESOLVED OpenCode plugin API (P1 research 2026-06-26 — pin verbatim in ADR 0004) ===
- Package: @opencode-ai/plugin v1.17.11 (peerDependency).
- Plugin LOAD PATHS (how OpenCode discovers a plugin — there is NO package.json manifest key like adlc-pi's 'pi' block; do not invent one): a plugin file in .opencode/plugins/ (project) or ~/.config/opencode/plugins/ (global), OR an npm package named in the "plugin" array of opencode.json. The deliverable installs via one of THESE paths and the smoke test asserts THAT path.
- Edit-interception hook: "tool.execute.before", async (input, output) => {...}. Throw `new Error(reason)` to DENY the tool call.
- VERIFY-FIRST EMPIRICAL FACTS (capture real OpenCode v1.17.11 payloads before coding; sources conflict; pin all answers in ADR 0004):
  (i) Arg shape: official docs read output.args.filePath; the johnlindquist gist reads input.args.filePath. Capture a real edit AND write payload, determine the true field, and PIN a captured fixture as the AC3 test input. Do not hand-mock the shape.
  (ii) DOES THROW ACTUALLY ABORT THE WRITE? (adversarial finding F4) Throwing in tool.execute.before is ASSUMED to prevent the edit, but this is unproven and the two sibling precedents diverge: adlc-codex throws-to-deny (prevent) while adlc-pi reverts AFTER the fact (index.ts:357 'automatically reverted'). Empirically determine whether a thrown error in tool.execute.before HALTS the write. If it only surfaces a message and the write proceeds, the control is theater — in that case fall back to a tool.execute.after detect-and-revert model (pi-style) and document the data-loss consideration (a revert can clobber concurrent legitimate changes). Pin the chosen model and the evidence in ADR 0004.
  (iii) Tool coverage: ENUMERATE every file-mutating tool name in v1.17.11 (candidates: edit, write, patch, multiedit, apply_patch) and cover each; document any (e.g. the bash tool) the hook cannot see → those fall to the CI backstop (see TWO-LAYER ENFORCEMENT).
  (iv) Workspace root: PIN which of the plugin context fields project/directory/worktree is the repo root used to locate .adlc/ and canonicalize paths; add a subdirectory-cwd test.
  (v) Loader: confirm OpenCode v1.17.11 executes plugin .ts directly (Bun) or ship a compiled index.js; the smoke test loads the SAME artifact OpenCode loads.
- Plugin shape: named export `export const adlcRailsGuard: Plugin = async ({ project, directory, worktree }) => ({ 'tool.execute.before': async (input) => {...} })`. Types via `import type` only.

=== RAIL-ENFORCEMENT CONTRACT — mirror the sibling hooks EXACTLY (do not improvise) ===
1. Active-ticket resolution: read process.env.ADLC_TICKET OR .adlc/current-ticket.json. Both set and conflicting => FAIL CLOSED (deny). See adlc-codex/hooks/adlc-rails-guard.mjs:296-304.
2. Enforcement is PHASE-SCOPED: only enforce when process.env.ADLC_P4_ENFORCEMENT === '1' (codex line 316); otherwise no-op.
3. Rails in force = the SINGLE active ticket's `rails` PLUS the implicit trust-root rails '.adlc/tickets.json' and '.adlc/current-ticket.json' (codex line 331; pi index.ts:49,65). NOT a union across all tickets; a different ticket's rails must NOT block.
4. No-op fail-safe in all of: .adlc/tickets.json absent; ADLC_P4_ENFORCEMENT != '1'; no active ticket resolved; active ticket has empty declared rails (trust-root files still frozen — match the sibling behavior precisely).
5. Path matching: port adlc-pi's globMatch(pattern, filePath) and canonicalizePath(filePath, cwd) helpers (exported for unit testing). On a rail match, deny (throw or revert per fact (ii)) naming the rail + ticket id.
6. Zero runtime dependencies in the deny hot path: Node builtins + inlined helpers only (`import type` for @opencode-ai/plugin). package.json may list @adlc/core / @adlc/rails-guard for parity, but the deny path must not import them.

=== TWO-LAYER ENFORCEMENT — the in-session hook is ADVISORY, the CI gate is MANDATORY (adversarial findings F1/F3) ===
The in-session hook is BYPASSABLE BY DESIGN: the agent controls its own environment, so it can leave ADLC_P4_ENFORCEMENT unset, or point ADLC_TICKET / .adlc/current-ticket.json at a rail-free ticket, and enforcement no-ops. This is acceptable ONLY because an unbypassable second layer exists. Therefore:
- The UNBYPASSABLE layer is the commit-time CI diff gate (docs/ci/rails-guard.yml driving scripts/rails-guard-ci.mjs), which is harness-agnostic (it inspects the git diff, so it already catches OpenCode-authored changes). The OpenCode integration MUST treat this as the real control, exactly as docs/integrations/claude-code.md does for Bash.
- docs/integrations/opencode.md MUST frame the two layers explicitly (advisory in-session hook + mandatory unbypassable CI gate) and link docs/ci/rails-guard.yml.
- ADR 0004 MUST contain a Threat Model section enumerating the in-session bypass surface (ADLC_P4_ENFORCEMENT unset; ADLC_TICKET env points at a rail-free ticket; a file-mutating tool the hook cannot see; shell-driven writes via the bash tool) and stating the CI gate as the mitigation.

=== DELIVERABLES ===
1. plugins/adlc-opencode/ — Node package: package.json (@adlc/opencode-package, type module, peerDependency @opencode-ai/plugin >=1.17.11), the index.ts plugin (and compiled index.js if the loader requires it), and a skills/ dir. Verify OpenCode's skill/command registration format against its docs FIRST; copy the sibling sentinels only if the format matches, else adapt and assert they load.
2. scripts/opencode-install-smoke.mjs — mirrors scripts/codex-install-smoke.mjs and scripts/claude-code-plugin-smoke.mjs: validate the plugin at its REAL OpenCode load path, the bundled hook, skill registration, and the hook zero-dependency guarantee. Must not require the opencode binary and must not mutate the user environment.
3. docs/integrations/opencode.md — real landing doc following codex.md / claude-code.md: Install, Usage, Formal ADLC Coverage table, the TWO-LAYER ENFORCEMENT framing above, Gaps, coexistence note, Boundary. Remove the 'no integration exists yet' banner.
4. docs/adr/0004-adlc-opencode-integration.md — ADR mirroring ADR 0003: decision, the pinned OpenCode plugin API (verbatim) and the resolved empirical facts (i)-(v), the per-primitive mapping, and the Threat Model section (above).

=== ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (each with a verification method) ===
- AC1: `node scripts/opencode-install-smoke.mjs .` exits 0 and reports the plugin (at its real OpenCode load path), hook, and skill registration valid. VERIFY: run it; assert exit 0.
- AC2: The deny path imports only Node builtins at runtime (types via `import type`). VERIFY: smoke-test dependency-guard assertion (mirror scripts/claude-code-plugin-smoke.mjs); a grep for non-builtin runtime import/require in the hook source returns nothing.
- AC3 (REQUIRED enforcement proof — runs by default, no opencode binary needed): a unit test at plugins/adlc-opencode/test/*.test.mjs invokes the REAL exported tool.execute.before handler (not a re-implementation) with the CAPTURED real payload shape, under `node --test`, covering and passing ALL of: (a) no-op when .adlc/tickets.json absent; (b) no-op when ADLC_P4_ENFORCEMENT != '1'; (c) no-op when no active ticket; (d) DENY an edit AND a write to the active ticket's declared rail; (e) DENY an edit to .adlc/tickets.json (trust-root) even when not in declared rails; (f) a DIFFERENT ticket's rail does NOT block; (g) conflicting ADLC_TICKET vs current-ticket.json fails closed. VERIFY: the test exercises each case via the real handler + real payload and exits 0.
- AC4: docs/integrations/opencode.md no longer contains 'no integration exists yet' and has an Install section, a Formal ADLC Coverage table, and a Gaps section. VERIFY: `grep -L 'no integration exists yet'` matches and a grep for the three headings returns 3.
- AC5: docs/adr/0004-adlc-opencode-integration.md exists, pins the resolved OpenCode plugin API, and has a Threat Model section. VERIFY: file exists and contains a fenced block naming @opencode-ai/plugin + version + tool.execute.before + the resolved filePath field + the enumerated mutating-tool names, AND a '## Threat Model' heading.
- AC6: No sibling integration regresses. VERIFY: `node scripts/codex-install-smoke.mjs .` and `node scripts/claude-code-plugin-smoke.mjs .` both still exit 0.
- AC7 (REQUIRED for GA — live deny proof; adversarial finding F2 — NOT optional/maintainer-only): an end-to-end check drives a real OpenCode edit to a declared rail with ADLC_P4_ENFORCEMENT=1 and asserts the edit is denied (or reverted per fact (ii)). It may be gated behind ADLC_OPENCODE_LIVE_INSTALL=1 for the disposable-env binary run, but a green GA REQUIRES this assertion to have been executed and recorded — it is a release gate, not an opt-in. VERIFY: the live check exists and, when run in the disposable env, asserts the deny; the ADR records the run.
- AC8 (two-layer enforcement; adversarial finding F1): docs/integrations/opencode.md states the in-session hook is advisory/bypassable and the CI gate (docs/ci/rails-guard.yml) is the mandatory unbypassable layer, and links it; and scripts/rails-guard-ci.mjs is shown to flag an OpenCode-authored diff that touches a frozen rail. VERIFY: grep opencode.md for the advisory framing + the docs/ci/rails-guard.yml link; and a test that runs scripts/rails-guard-ci.mjs against a synthetic diff touching a rail exits non-zero (gate fails as intended).

Suppressions are denied. The one allowed pivot: if OpenCode v1.17.11 genuinely exposes no usable edit-interception or post-edit hook (contradicting the resolved API), document in ADR 0004 that in-session enforcement is unavailable and rail protection relies ENTIRELY on the mandatory CI gate, and mark AC3/AC7 deny-cases not-applicable with justification — the CI gate (AC8) remains required regardless.
